title: Understanding the Problem
text: A square has equal sides, and the diagonal forms a right triangle with two sides of the square. Use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the length of each side.

title: Setting Up the Equation
text: In a right triangle, the Pythagorean Theorem states that $$a^2+b^2=c^2$$, where c is the hypotenuse. Here, the diagonal is the hypotenuse.

title: Formulating the Equation
text: Since the sides of the square are equal, let each side be a. The equation becomes $$a^2+a^2={10}^2$$. Simplify this equation.

title: Solving for a
text: Divide both sides of the equation by $$2$$ to isolate $$a^2$$.

title: Finding the Length of a Side
text: Take the square root of both sides to find the length of a.
